summary: First comprehensive test pass on AgencyOS MVP. Tested backend via pytest (11 tests) and critical frontend flows via Playwright: admin login, CRM lead creation, Won-Automation (Lead->Client+Project+Invoice+Tasks+Timeline+Notification), Client Portal creation & login, RBAC role separation (frontend nav + backend endpoints), Stripe checkout end-to-end, AI Assistant streaming, Command Palette, Vault. Core happy-path flows (auth, won-automation, portal creation/login, RBAC frontend routing, AI streaming) all work correctly and are genuinely well-built. Found 3 real backend bugs and 1 frontend integration bug detailed below.

critical.issue: Stripe success_url/cancel_url built from `request.headers.get('origin')` instead of the configured FRONTEND_URL env var. In this preview environment the browser's Origin header resolves to an internal cluster domain (e.g. agency-os-31.cluster-5.preview.emergentcf.cloud) rather than the public preview URL. After a successful Stripe test payment (card 4242...), the redirect lands on a '403 Forbidden' page instead of the app, so the frontend polling logic (checkout/status/{session_id}) never runs and the invoice stays 'draft' even though Stripe/backend already marked the payment_transaction and would mark invoice 'paid' if the status endpoint were called. Verified: manually calling GET /api/invoices/checkout/status/{session_id} after the broken redirect correctly flipped invoice status to 'paid'. Fix: use os.environ['FRONTEND_URL'] for success_url/cancel_url instead of request-derived origin.

Lockout key is `f'{request.client.host}:{email}'`. Behind this environment's ingress/proxy, request.client.host varies between requests to the SAME public URL (observed two different internal IPs 10.208.128.2 and 10.208.128.6 alternating across consecutive login attempts), splitting the failed-attempt counter so it never reaches 5 and 429 is never returned through the public URL - confirmed via direct curl loop (7 attempts, all 401, counts split 4/3 across 2 identifiers in DB). When hit directly on localhost:8001 (single consistent IP) lockout works correctly (6th attempt -> 429). This means brute-force protection is effectively bypassed for real internet traffic through the ingress. Fix: key on email alone, or use X-Forwarded-For with trust-appropriate logic, not raw request.client.host.

Uses `Depends(get_current_user)` instead of `require_admin`/`require_staff`. Any authenticated user including a `client` role portal user can call this endpoint directly and receive the full team member list (names/emails/roles) - confirmed via test: client session GET returned 200 with team data instead of expected 403.

minor.issue: Backend returns correct results (verified via curl: searching 'PWTest' correctly returns matching lead, client, project), but the frontend Command Palette (Cmd/Ctrl+K) did not display any matching results for the same query in 2 separate Playwright runs ('No results found.' shown once, palette closed unexpectedly on a retry). Likely a frontend integration/debounce/state bug rather than backend.

ui_bugs.issue: Console warning on every login: 'Cannot update a component while rendering a different component... BrowserRouter Login Login' - React anti-pattern (likely a setState call happening during render, e.g. calling navigate() or setState directly in render body instead of useEffect). Not blocking but should be cleaned up.

integration_issues.issue: See critical backend issue above - user lands on a 403 Forbidden blank page after successfully paying via Stripe test checkout instead of returning to the invoice with a 'paid' status. This breaks the entire client-facing payment confirmation UX.
Typing a query (e.g. 'PWTest') that matches an existing lead/client/project (confirmed present via direct API call) does not reliably render matching quick-action/search results in the palette UI.
affected_selectors: No data-testid found on the command palette container itself; could not locate via [data-testid="command-palette"]

action_items: Fix Stripe checkout success_url/cancel_url to use FRONTEND_URL env var, not request Origin header (critical - breaks payment confirmation flow)
Fix brute-force lockout identifier to not rely solely on request.client.host behind the proxy (critical security gap)
Restrict GET /api/settings/team to require_admin or require_staff (client role can currently see team member list)
Investigate Command Palette not showing search results despite backend /api/search returning correct data; add data-testid="command-palette" to the dialog container for testability
Clean up React setState-during-render warning on Login page (minor code quality)
critical_code_review_comments: auth.py/auth_utils.py: bcrypt hashing ($2b$), httpOnly+secure+samesite cookies, explicit CORS origin (app-level, correct) all implemented well - good security baseline.

automation_engine.run_won_automation is well-structured, idempotent (verified: re-marking an already-won lead as 'won' again does NOT re-trigger automation, old_stage check works correctly), and creates a consistent audit trail (activity log + automation log + notification).
vault.py correctly excludes encrypted_password/password_hash/two_fa_secret from all list/create/update responses and only decrypts on-demand via the /reveal endpoint with audit logging - good design.
portal.py consistently enforces client_id ownership on every query - no cross-tenant data leakage observed in the routes reviewed.
Minor: server.py CORS_ORIGINS env var in .env is unused (code hardcodes to FRONTEND_URL only) - either remove CORS_ORIGINS from .env or wire it in for multi-origin support consistency.

success_rate.backend: 73% (8/11 pytest passed; 3 genuine app bugs found, not test bugs)
success_rate.frontend: ~90% of tested flows passed (login, CRM lead creation, won-automation UI+toast, client detail/checklist/portal-creation, portal RBAC redirect, portal ticket creation, Stripe checkout redirect-to-Stripe, AI Assistant streaming); Stripe post-payment redirect and Command Palette search failed

test_credentials: admin@obrinex.com / AgencyOS@2026 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Dynamically created during this test run: portal user portaltest_1783281774@pwtest.com / v_PgTXiZVqo (linked to client 'PWTest Co 1783281691', client_id 6a4ab856650256a27c4a1053). Test lead/client/project/invoice(s) created with 'TEST_'/'PWTest'/'TEST' prefixes were NOT cleaned up - recommend main agent or next tester purges test_database collections (leads, clients, projects, invoices, tasks, tickets, users) with company_name/email containing 'PWTest', 'TEST_', 'bfcheck', 'bruteforce', 'brutetest' before further testing.
seed_data_creation: Created via UI/API during testing: 1 lead->won->client->project->invoice chain ('PWTest Co ...'), 1 client portal user, 1 support ticket, multiple brute-force test login attempts (some accounts now in login_attempts collection).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Won-automation, RBAC frontend routing, portal creation/login, AI Assistant streaming, and Stripe checkout session creation are all confirmed WORKING correctly - do not need to be re-verified from scratch. Focus retest on: (1) Stripe post-payment redirect after main agent fixes success_url to use FRONTEND_URL, (2) brute-force lockout after fixing the IP-key issue - test via direct backend curl loop with same email 6+ times, (3) settings/team RBAC after adding require_admin, (4) Command Palette search rendering. Not yet tested due to time/scope: Projects Kanban/List toggle, Tasks page, Finance expense creation, Invoices manual creation/send, Proposals AI generation streaming, Knowledge Base, Files upload/download/delete, Automations log viewing, Analytics charts, Settings company/2FA/invite-team/audit-logs, notifications bell. These should be covered in the next pass.
rca_of_the_issue: Stripe redirect bug: root cause is `origin = request.headers.get('origin') or f'https://{request.headers.get("host")}'` in finance.py's create_checkout - this trusts client-supplied/proxy-forwarded headers instead of the server's own known FRONTEND_URL config, which is unreliable behind multi-hop ingress/proxy layers common in preview/staging Kubernetes environments. Brute-force bug: root cause is using `request.client.host` (TCP peer address as seen by the FastAPI process) as part of the rate-limit key; behind a load-balancing ingress, this is the ingress pod's IP, not the real client IP, and multiple ingress replicas produce different fluctuating IPs per request, defeating any host-based counting without X-Forwarded-For support.
